channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawmakers press Pentagon to re-ground Ospreys until safety issues can be addressed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three lawmakers are asking Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin to consider re-grounding the military’s fleet of V-22 Ospreys until solutions can be put in place to address safety and design issues identified by The Associated Press in its recent in-depth investigation of the aircraft’s
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Minnesota abortion access in federal court
item.description: Minnesota has some of the most accommodating laws granting access to abortion. A lawsuit filed late last week seeks to upend them.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Anna Haecherl, MPR News

item.title: Gavin Newsom threatens intervention if Trump kills Biden's $7.5K EV tax credit
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom revealed that he would offer Californians a $7,500 tax credit for electric vehicles if President-elect Trump gets rid of the rebate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Commerce Department to reduce Intel's funding on semiconductors
item.description: The Biden administration plans on reducing part of Intel’s $8.5 billion in federal funding for computer chip plants around the country, according to three people familiar with the grant who spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss private conversations.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats plan to elect new party leader just days after Trump's inauguration
item.description: The chair of the Democratic National Committee has informed party leaders that the organization will elect his successor just days after Donald Trump is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House insists Biden, Harris have 'one of most successful administrations in history' despite 2024 loss
item.description: The White House released a memo hailing the Biden-Harris administration as one of the most successful in history, despite losing the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Special Counsel Smith seeks to dismiss Jan. 6 charges, appeal in classified records case against Trump
item.description: Special Counsel Jack Smith is asking a judge to drop all charges against President-elect Donald Trump stemming from Smith's investigation into the Jan. 6 U.S. Capitol breach, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge halts cannabis license lottery that is precursor to Minnesota marijuana retail launch
item.description: A Ramsey County judge blocked a regulatory agency from holding a license pre-approval lottery after applicants who were denied entry sued the Office of Cannabis Management over the process.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. emerges as a political force of his own as he helps his father launch a second term
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. has become a prominent player in his father’s political orbit and the Make America Great Again movement.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At the crossroads of news and opinion, 'Morning Joe' hosts grapple with aftermath of Trump meeting
item.description: The reaction of those who defended “Morning Joe” hosts Joe Scarborough and Mika Brzezinski for meeting with President-elect Trump sounds almost quaint in the days of opinionated journalism.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's Republican Party is increasingly winning union voters. It's a shift seen in his labor pick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Working-class voters helped Republicans make steady election gains this year and expanded a coalition that increasingly includes rank-and-file union members, a political shift spotlighting one of President-elect Donald Trump’s latest Cabinet picks: a GOP congresswoman, who has draw
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's top military commander calls out US senator for questioning a woman's role in combat
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — The first woman to command Canada's military called out a U.S. senator on Saturday for questioning the role of women in combat. Gen. Jennie Carignan responded to comments made by Idaho Republican Sen. Jim Risch, the ranking member of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Applicants sue Minnesota’s Office of Cannabis Management over disqualification
item.description: The OCM turned to a lottery because more than 1,800 applicants had sought 282 licenses. This week, regulators disqualified two-thirds of the applicants for a variety of reasons.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Feven Gerezgiher, MPR News

item.title: The 61st anniversary of JFK's assassination
item.description: More than six decades since the assassination of President John F. Kennedy, questions remain as to what truly occurred.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Senate reaches deal on stalled Biden judicial nominees
item.description: The Senate announced a deal Thursday to complete work on seven of President Biden’s nine stalled judicial nominees, who sparked an intense fight on the Senate floor this week.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names former Florida AG Pam Bondi as his new pick for U.S. attorney general
item.description: After former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration, President-elect Donald Trump named Pam Bondi, a former attorney general of Florida, as his next pick for U.S. attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Tabke wins Shakopee-area legislative race after recount, House control still unknown
item.description: The results are unofficial because four ballots are still in dispute. Tabke’s margin of victory widened by one vote from an earlier count.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Report: Minnesota Lottery needs to tighten up how it regulates retailers
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of the Legislative Auditor reports that the Minnesota State Lottery generally complies with requirements, but has room to improve when it comes to how it manages retailers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hennepin County commissioners delay vote on proposal to reduce jail population
item.description: The state’s Department of Corrections ordered Hennepin County to reduce the number of inmates at some of its jails by Dec. 5.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: House Ethics Committee is deadlocked on whether to release Gaetz report
item.description: The U.S. House Committee on Ethics is deadlocked on whether to release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz, whose nomination to serve as Trump’s attorney general has been plagued by controversy.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh and Lexie Schapitl, MPR News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9
